docs.title: RGFX Documentation
docs.text: Welcome to the RGFX documentation. RGFX (Retro Game Effects) is a framework that creates external visual effects for retro video games.

docs.title: What is RGFX?
docs.text: RGFX connects your retro gaming setup to LED lighting effects. When you play classic arcade games in MAME, game events like scoring points, losing lives, or defeating enemies trigger real-world LED effects on strips and matrices connected to ESP32 microcontrollers.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
The RGFX system consists of three main components:

  • RGFX Hub - The desktop application that coordinates everything
  • ESP32 Drivers - Microcontroller firmware that controls LED hardware
  • MAME Interceptors - Lua scripts that monitor game state and generate events

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Ready to set up your own retro gaming light show? Head to the Getting Started guide.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide will help you set up RGFX for the first time.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• RGFX Hub - macOS application (download from releases)
  • ESP32 microcontroller - ESP32-C3 or ESP32-S3 recommended
  • LED hardware - WS2812B LED strips or matrices
  • MAME - Version 0.250 or later

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
1. Install RGFX Hub - Download and install the Hub application
  2. Flash a Driver - Use the Hub to flash firmware to your ESP32
  3. Configure LEDs - Set up your LED strip or matrix configuration
  4. Connect to MAME - Install interceptors for your favorite games
  5. Play! - Start a game and watch the lights react

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: Detailed guides for each step will be added here.
